socrates.thinking:
Good news! After the system_reset, the first screenshot (vm-bios1.ppm) shows 640x480 resolution with multiple colors - this looks like the BIOS/POST screen with a blue Windows-like logo! This confirms the VGA output is working correctly.

The second screenshot (vm-bios2.ppm) shows 720x400 with mostly black and 18 gray pixels - this is likely the text mode boot screen or a transition between BIOS and DOS boot.

Let me wait longer for the VM to fully boot. Windows 3.11 on DOS should take some time. I need to keep taking screenshots to see the progression

thinking.text:
Good news! After the system_reset, the first screenshot (vm-bios1.ppm) shows 640x480 resolution with multiple colors - this looks like the BIOS/POST screen with a blue Windows-like logo! This confirms the VGA output is working correctly.

The second screenshot (vm-bios2.ppm) shows 720x400 with mostly black and 18 gray pixels - this is likely the text mode boot screen or a transition between BIOS and DOS boot.

Let me wait longer for the VM to fully boot. Windows 3.11 on DOS should take some time. I need to keep taking screenshots to see the progression.
